perturbation.original_text: 11. Length of Agreement 11.1. This agreement shall be in full force upon its execution by the Parties and shall be and effect inde finitely. For greater clarity and certainty, the obligations herein with respect to Confidential Information continue indefinitely and beyond the time limit of the specific engagement for whi ch the Town engaged the Provider .
perturbation.changed_text: 11. Length of Agreement 11.1. This agreement shall be in full force upon its execution by the Parties and shall be in effect for a period of one (1) year, automatically renewing for subsequent one-year terms unless either party provides written notice of termination at least thirty (30) days prior to the end of the then-current term. For greater clarity and certainty, the obligations herein with respect to Confidential Information continue indefinitely and beyond the time limit of the specific engagement for whi ch the Town engaged the Provider.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text introduces an in-text contradiction by specifying a fixed term of one year with automatic renewals for the agreement as a whole, while simultaneously stating that obligations regarding Confidential Information continue indefinitely. This contradicts the defined term of the contract, making it unclear whether the confidentiality obligations are tied to the one-year term or truly indefinite, which leads to uncertainty in enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 11. Length of Agreement 11.1. This agreement shall be in full force upon its execution by the Parties and shall be in effect for a period of one (1) year, automatically renewing for subsequent one-year terms unless either party provides written notice of termination at least thirty (30) days prior to the end of the then-current term. For greater clarity and certainty, the obligations herein with respect to Confidential Information continue indefinitely and beyond the time limit of the specific engagement for whi ch the Town engaged the Provider.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies Section 11.1 to specify a one-year term with automatic renewals, while also stating that confidentiality obligations continue indefinitely. This creates a contradiction because the agreement as a whole has a defined term, but specific obligations related to Confidential Information do not, leading to uncertainty about whether those obligations are tied to the term or truly indefinite. The contradicted text is the changed text itself since the contradiction is within the section.

perturbation.original_text: 4.1. The Provider will agree to use their best efforts to prevent disclosure of Confidential Information to any third party for an indefinite period of time from the date of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.1. The Provider will agree to use commercially reasonable efforts to prevent disclosure of Confidential Information to any third party for a period of five (5) years from the date of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates the Provider to use their best efforts to prevent disclosure for an indefinite period. The change limits the obligation to 'commercially reasonable efforts' and a defined period of five years. This contradicts the indefinite term mentioned in section 11.1, creating an in-text contradiction regarding the duration of confidentiality obligations. It is now unclear if it is indefinite or 5 years.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 11. Length of Agreement 11.1. This agreement shall be in full force upon its execution by the Parties and shall be and effect inde finitely. For greater clarity and certainty, the obligations herein with respect to Confidential Information continue indefinitely and beyond the time limit of the specific engagement for whi ch the Town engaged the Provider .
perturbation.justification: The original text requires 'best efforts' for an 'indefinite period' to prevent disclosure of confidential information. The changed text modifies this to 'commercially reasonable efforts' for a 'period of five (5) years'. Section 11.1 of the contract states that obligations regarding confidential information continue 'indefinitely'. The changed text directly contradicts this indefinite obligation by limiting it to 5 years, creating an in-text contradiction as per the provided definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: 4.5. The Provider shall immediately notify the Town of any security breach (including any internal unauthorized use or disclosure), investigate the security breach, and take measures to reme diate such breach at the Vendor s cost as directed by the Town.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.5. The Provider shall notify the Town of any security breach (including any internal unauthorized use or disclosure) within 30 days of discovery, investigate the security breach, and take measures to remediate such breach at a mutually agreed upon cost.
perturbation.explanation: The changed text provides a 30-day window for notifying the Town of a security breach, conflicting with the original requirement for 'immediate' notification. Additionally, it changes the cost remediation to 'mutually agreed upon cost', creating an in-text contradiction with the original text that specified that the vendor would cover the cost of the remediation. This change creates uncertainty in enforcement because now the vendor may not pay for the damages, and it's also unclear what immediate notification is.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.5. The Provider shall notify the Town of any security breach (including any internal unauthorized use or disclosure) within 30 days of discovery, investigate the security breach, and take measures to remediate such breach at a mutually agreed upon cost.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the notification period from 'immediately' to 'within 30 days', and alters the cost responsibility from the Vendor's cost to a 'mutually agreed upon cost'. This directly contradicts the original requirement of immediate notification and the vendor covering remediation costs, creating an inconsistency as defined by the perturbation type and is repeated in the contradicted text making it a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 1.1. All information disclosed by the Town in oral, written, graphic, photographic, recorded, prototype, sample or in any other form that is related to the Information Technology , Geographic Information Systems (GIS) and Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition (SCADA) systems for Town of The Blue Mountains or any information written, graphic, photographic, recorded, prototype, sample or in any other form that is generated by the Provider for the purpose of doing business with The Town shall be consi dered Confidential Information. Any information considered Private Information by the Municipal Freedom of Information and Protection of Privacy Act (MFIPPA) , any personal health information as defined by the Personal Health Information Protection Act (PHIPA), and by the Personal Information Protection and Electronic Documents Act (PIPEDA) is also considered Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.1. All information disclosed by the Town in oral, written, graphic, photographic, recorded, prototype, sample or in any other form that is related to the Information Technology, Geographic Information Systems (GIS) and Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition (SCADA) systems for Town of The Blue Mountains shall be considered Confidential Information. Information generated by the Provider is not Confidential Information. Any information considered Private Information by the Municipal Freedom of Information and Protection of Privacy Act (MFIPPA), any personal health information as defined by the Personal Health Information Protection Act (PHIPA), and by the Personal Information Protection and Electronic Documents Act (PIPEDA) is also considered Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text explicitly excludes information generated by the Provider from being considered Confidential Information. This directly contradicts the original definition, which included information generated by the Provider for the purpose of doing business with the Town. It also contradicts later sections relying on this definition, such as the return of Confidential Information (6.1). Now it's not clear what the provider can return, which creates in-text contradictions in enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6.1. The Town may, at any time, request the return of all Confidential Information (including notes generated by the Provider on Confidential Information given orally) and all copies thereof, received from or on behalf of the Town , and the Provider agrees to promptly comply with such requests. The Provider agrees that, subsequent to a request for return of Confidential Information or notification of termination of business discussions and/or collaboration, Confidential Information provided orally will continue to be kept confidential by the Provider and the provisions of this Agreement shall continue with respect to all Confidential Information until an y of items 3.1 a), b) and c) become applicable. If the information is unable to be returned then information must be destroyed and a certificate of destruction must be issued.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly states that 'Information generated by the Provider is not Confidential Information,' directly contradicting the original text's definition, which included 'information generated by the Provider for the purpose of doing business with The Town' as Confidential Information. This creates a direct conflict with section 6.1, which requires the return of 'all Confidential Information (including notes generated by the Provider on Confidential Information given orally).' Since the changed text now excludes the provider's information, the provider doesn't have to return it, which contradicts 6.1. This is a strong in-text contradiction based on the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction.'
